question: Looking at the US median income map, which statement below is the most accurate?

options.A: The median income differences are impossible to determine from this visualization.
options.B: The median income gap between Oklahoma and Illinois might exceeds that between California and North Carolina.
options.C: All states have very diverse median income levels.
options.D: Oklahoma and Illinois have similar median incomes.

explanation: Oklahoma (56.0$k) and Illinois (59.0$k) have a 3.0$k median income difference, while California (64.7$k) and North Carolina (66.5$k) have only a 1.8$k difference. However, the categorical color encoding groups Oklahoma and Illinois into the same color bin (55-65$k range), making their large difference appear similar, while California and North Carolina appear more different due to different color bins.
